competitors.label: Computed Properties
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical Vue page for computed refs, caching, getters and setters, and side-effect-free best practices.
theirStrengths: The docs are strong on dependency tracking, side-effect-free getters, and the 'derived snapshot' mental model.
theirGaps: They are less interview-shaped and lighter on compact production pitfall framing.

realWorldUseCases: Practical scenario You compute a cart total from line items and want it to update only when inputs change.
actionableExamples: Worked scenario: computed vs method in a real component A cart drawer renders subtotal and filtered line items.

realWorldUseCases: The docs explain why computed values update when dependencies change, but they are less focused on one short production scenario.
actionableExamples: They include clear getter, writable computed, and side-effect guidance examples, so example quality stays close.
followUpConfusion: The page covers important follow-ups like pure getters and not mutating computed snapshots, so the follow-up criterion remains close.
Add one explicit line that computed should stay synchronous and pure, and defer side effects to watch.

competitors.winReason: The official docs are canonical on computed behavior, but our page now adds a tighter worked example showing the method-versus-computed trade-off inside a real component.

competitors.label: Vue Interview Questions
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd's Vue interview bank is directly relevant because computed properties and reactivity fundamentals are core interview topics.
theirStrengths: It is a broad interview-prep source with structured Vue question coverage and worked solutions.
theirGaps: The landing page is not dedicated to computed properties, so it is less explicit about caching and getter/setter follow-ups.

realWorldUseCases: Practical scenario You compute a cart total from line items and want it to update only when inputs change.
followUpConfusion: Common pitfalls Using methods instead of computed, causing unnecessary recalculation. Mutating state inside computed getters. Forgetting computed must return a value.

realWorldUseCases: The Vue bank is relevant at the interview-topic level, but it is not as focused on this exact derived-state scenario.
actionableExamples: Because GreatFrontEnd emphasizes worked answers, the example bar remains reasonably competitive.
followUpConfusion: The category page is less explicit about caching, setter support, and common getter mistakes than a dedicated computed answer.

actionableExamples: Add one tighter getter-versus-method snippet instead of relying mostly on prose.

competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd is useful as a broad Vue interview resource, but our page is more focused on concrete computed-property pitfalls and the derived-state mental model.

competitors.label: Computed Properties
competitors.selectionReason: Vue School has a topic-direct lesson on computed properties and is a strong tutorial/reference comparator.
theirStrengths: It is a dedicated computed-properties lesson and clearly positions them as reusable up-to-date values in templates.
theirGaps: The visible lesson summary is lighter on setters, production pitfalls, and interview follow-ups.

realWorldUseCases: Practical scenario You compute a cart total from line items and want it to update only when inputs change.
actionableExamples: Computed Getters and Setters Computed properties can also include a setter to handle updates in both directions.

realWorldUseCases: The lesson explains computed properties as reusable transformed data, but the visible framing is less grounded in a specific production scenario.
actionableExamples: It is a lesson with examples, but the surfaced summary is lighter on concrete setter and pitfall examples than our page.
followUpConfusion: The lesson summary is less explicit about common mistakes like mutating state inside getters or forgetting to return a value.
Add one compact line contrasting computed with watch so the follow-up path is clearer.

competitors.winReason: Vue School is a direct comparator, but our page gives a slightly more reusable interview answer by pairing the core concept with setters and a short pitfall checklist.
